expectations.text: workflow.md validates cleanly via `markflow init` (exit 0, no parse/validation errors)
expectations.evidence: markflow init exited 0; workspace created without errors

expectations.text: File contains an `# Inputs` section declaring a `city` parameter
expectations.evidence: Line 17-19: `# Inputs\n- city (required): Name of the city...`

expectations.text: `# Flow` section present with exactly one fenced mermaid block
expectations.evidence: Lines 21-27: flowchart TD with fetch_weather --> recommend --> render

expectations.text: `# Steps` section present; every node referenced in the flow has a matching `## <id>` heading and every `## <id>` heading appears in the flow
expectations.evidence: Nodes {fetch_weather, recommend, render} all have ## headings; no orphan headings

expectations.text: Includes at least one script step (```bash / ```sh / ```python / ```js) that performs the weather fetch
expectations.evidence: ## fetch_weather uses ```bash to call geocoding-api.open-meteo.com and api.open-meteo.com

expectations.text: Includes at least one agent step (plain prose under a `##` heading, no fenced code block body) for the dog-walk recommendation
expectations.evidence: ## recommend (lines 98-114) is plain prose, no code fence

expectations.text: The agent step references the city via Liquid templating (e.g., `{{ city }}` or `{{ GLOBAL.* }}` populated from the fetch step) — not hard-coded
expectations.evidence: Line 101: `{{ GLOBAL.weather.city }}` and weather fields via `{{ GLOBAL.weather.* }}`

expectations.text: No use of banned patterns: `local`/`global` keys nested inside `RESULT`, unpaired `max:N`/`:max` edges, or unknown code-block language
expectations.evidence: No RESULT nesting, no retry edges, only bash code blocks
